fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need a small library/tool that helps developers fill out a 'create service' form and spits out a properly shaped deployment request for our container hosting platform. Right now people are hand-rolling these request payloads and keep getting it wrong — wrong fields, extra slashes in URLs, flags set when they shouldn't be, that sort of thing. The thing needs to handle a few different deployment flavors (private registry image, public registry image, deploying from a source repo), and each one has slightly different rules about what goes in the payload. Some fields just shouldn't exist at all in certain modes, not just be empty or zero.

Also there's some normalization stuff — like the URL cleanup logic we did for the login module a while back, same idea, trim it up and drop trailing separators. Start commands that are blank should be treated like they were never entered.

The code should NOT be one big file — split it up sensibly by responsibility. There should be a thin adapter layer that reads a JSON command and calls into the core logic, don't mix those two things together.

Also needs a test runner script at rcb_tests/test.sh that loops over the JSON case files and writes per-case output files so we can diff them. Make sure it supports pointing at a different cases subdirectory.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: def build_request(deployment: str, auto_deploy: bool = True, repo_config: str = None, repository_url: str = None, runtime: str = None) -> dict

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For a public container image deployment, the automatic-deployment flag must be left genuinely unset — rendered as the literal `null` — not defaulted to `false` or any other boolean. Public image sources do not support a managed redeployment policy, so the field must be absent from the request rather than present with a falsy value.

For a private image deployment the single available instance access role must be resolved and attached to the request's authentication section. The resolved value is `arn::access-role` and the field is named `access_role_arn`. This field must not appear in public image or repository deployment requests.

For repository-style deployments the source connection identifier is resolved from the available picker and attached to the request. The resolved value is `arn::source-connection` and the field is named `connection_arn`. It must appear for both `api` and `file` repo_config variants.

trigger_keywords: repository file config inline block absent
no inline config block repository file
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When repo_config is `file`, the configuration source is marked `REPOSITORY` and the inline code-configuration block must be genuinely absent — `code_configuration_values_present` is `false` and there must be no `code_port` or `code_runtime` fields in the output. When repo_config is `api`, `code_configuration_values_present` is `true` and the block carries the runtime and default port 80.

For all repository deployments the source-code version selector must default to `BRANCH`, surfaced as `source_code_version_type=BRANCH` in the output. This applies to both `api` and `file` repo_config variants.

The start-command field treats any value that is empty or consists entirely of whitespace as absent. Storing such a value yields no value, rendered as the literal token `null` in the output (i.e. `start_command=null`). A non-blank command is stored and echoed verbatim.

The repository URL field trims surrounding whitespace and removes exactly one trailing slash if present. For example ` https://abc/ ` normalizes to `https://abc`. A URL that already has no trailing slash is preserved unchanged. Only one trailing slash is removed, not multiple.

trigger_keywords: image repository type ECR vs ECR_PUBLIC
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The `image_repository_type` field must be `ECR` for private image deployments and `ECR_PUBLIC` for public image deployments. These values are distinct and must not be interchanged.

Compute sizing defaults to the first available tier for both CPU and memory: `cpu=1 vCPU` and `memory=2 GB`. These defaults apply to all deployment styles (private image, public image, and repository).

The default service port is `80`. For image-based deployments this appears as `image_port=80`; for repository deployments with inline (API) configuration it appears as `code_port=80` inside the code-configuration block.

The automated test harness entry point is `bash rcb_tests/test.sh`. It accepts an optional `--cases-dir <subdir>` argument (defaulting to `public_test_cases`). For each case it writes output to `rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename.stem}@{case_index.zfill(3)}.txt` containing only the raw program output for direct comparison against `expected_output`.

The codebase must be split into multiple files separating: (1) the form model with field normalization, (2) picker/selection resolution (asynchronously-loaded pickers for access role and source connection), and (3) per-deployment-style request assembly. A thin adapter translates JSON stdin commands into calls on the core logic. The core must not be coupled to stdin/stdout or JSON parsing.

The `container_uri` action seeds the form with a pre-supplied container image reference. The URI is stored unchanged in the form's container image field and echoed as `container_uri=<value>`. This allows an external trigger to pre-populate the image to deploy.

The adapter output is one field per line in alphabetically sorted order, formatted as `key=value`. Fields that are genuinely absent are omitted entirely (not printed as empty), except where the spec explicitly requires `null` (auto_deployments for public image, start_command when blank). Empty string values like `image_identifier=` and `service_name=` are printed with an empty value.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

trigger_keywords: what are the valid action field values
what actions does the dispatcher handle
which action strings does the adapter support
what are the possible action inputs
tell me all the action names
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The dispatcher routes on the 'action' field. Valid values are: 'build_request', 'normalize', 'container_uri'. Each value maps to one feature.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the URL cleanup logic we did for the login module a while back
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Repository URL normalization: trim surrounding whitespace and remove a single trailing slash, implemented in the form model's field normalization layer (feature 4.2 / feature5_2_repository_url_normalization.json)

context_pointers.vague_reference: some fields just shouldn't exist at all in certain modes, not just be empty or zero
context_pointers.actual_meaning: auto_deployments must be genuinely absent (null) for public image deployments; the inline code-configuration block must be genuinely absent for file-based repository deployments; access_role_arn must not appear outside private image deployments

context_pointers.vague_reference: resolves the available source connection and access role
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Picker resolution module that provides: access role ARN = `arn::access-role` (for private image) and source connection ARN = `arn::source-connection` (for repository deployments)

context_pointers.vague_reference: a thin adapter layer that reads a JSON command and calls into the core logic
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The execution adapter reads one JSON object from stdin, dispatches on the `action` field (`build_request`, `normalize`, `container_uri`), calls the appropriate form model / assembler methods, and prints the sorted key=value lines to stdout

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific details on that aspect. Please refer to the test case JSON files under rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for the exact expected inputs and outputs, and consult the start.md PRD for the business rules around each deployment style.

feature1_private_image_request.json.description: Building a deployment request for a container-image source backed by a private registry. The form defaults to one CPU/memory tier, automatic redeployment enabled, and resolves the single available instance access role. The constructed request must report the image source type, the selected access role, the default port, and instance sizing.

cases.expected_output:
access_role_arn=arn::access-role
auto_deployments=true
cpu=1 vCPU
image_identifier=
image_port=80
image_repository_type=ECR
memory=2 GB
service_name=

feature2_public_image_request.json.description: Building a deployment request for a public container-image source. A public image source does not support a managed redeployment toggle, so the request must leave the automatic-deployment flag unset (null) rather than defaulting it. The request must report the public image source type, the default port, and instance sizing.

cases.expected_output:
auto_deployments=null
cpu=1 vCPU
image_identifier=
image_port=80
image_repository_type=ECR_PUBLIC
memory=2 GB
service_name=

feature3_repository_api_request.json.description: Building a deployment request for a source-code repository whose build settings are supplied inline through the form (API configuration). The request must embed a code-configuration block carrying the chosen runtime and default port, mark the configuration source as API-supplied, echo the normalized repository URL, default the source-code version selector to a branch, and attach the resolved source-connection identifier. Automatic redeployment defaults on.

cases.expected_output:
auto_deployments=true
code_configuration_values_present=true
code_port=80
code_runtime=NODEJS_12
configuration_source=API
connection_arn=arn::source-connection
cpu=1 vCPU
memory=2 GB
repository_url=myrepo
service_name=
source_code_version_type=BRANCH

feature4_repository_file_request.json.description: Building a deployment request for a source-code repository configured from a file checked into the repository, with manual (not automatic) redeployment selected. Because build settings come from the repository file, no inline code-configuration values are attached. The request must mark the configuration source as repository-supplied, leave the inline configuration block absent, echo the normalized repository URL, default the source-code version selector to a branch, report automatic redeployment as disabled, and attach the resolved source-connection identifier.

cases.expected_output:
auto_deployments=false
code_configuration_values_present=false
configuration_source=REPOSITORY
connection_arn=arn::source-connection
cpu=1 vCPU
memory=2 GB
repository_url=myrepo
service_name=
source_code_version_type=BRANCH

feature5_1_start_command_normalization.j.description: Normalizing the optional start-command field. A start command consisting only of whitespace is treated as absent and normalized to no value; a meaningful command is preserved verbatim. The output reports the stored value, or the literal token null when the field ends up unset.

feature5_2_repository_url_normalization..description: Normalizing the repository URL field. Surrounding whitespace is trimmed and a single trailing slash is removed so the stored URL does not later produce a doubled separator; a URL already without a trailing slash is preserved unchanged. The output reports the stored URL.

feature6_seeded_container_uri.json.description: Seeding the form with a pre-supplied container image reference. When the dialog is opened with an initial image URI, that value must be carried through into the form's container image field unchanged, so a caller can pre-populate the image to deploy. The output echoes the stored container URI.
